headlines.title: CVS Caremark will cover Lilly’s weight loss drug Zepbound again after patient backlash
headlines.description: CVS Caremark will resume covering the weight loss drug Zepbound this year after it removed it from its list of covered medications last year, drugmaker Eli Lilly said.

headlines.title: When drug after drug failed, a teen hoped brain surgery could fix her daily seizures. Insurance denied it.
headlines.description: Several weeks after NBC News reached out to Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield about the denial, the insurer changed its policy to include coverage of deep brain stimulation for certain children.

headlines.title: At the Enhanced Games, drugs don’t get athletes banned. They could get them rich.
headlines.description: When Shania Collins was first approached about taking performance-enhancing drugs last year, it made her nervous enough to contact two members of the Drug Enforcement Administration — her parents.

headlines.title: Absence of USAID likely slowed Ebola detection and response, former officials say
headlines.description: The Trump administration hollowed out USAID last year, but the State Department denied the changes have hampered its response.

headlines.title: From Omaha Steaks to hazmat suits: What life was like for contagion patients in quarantine
headlines.description: As more than 40 Americans remain in quarantine for up to six weeks following a hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship, former patients who spent time inside some of the country’s highest-security medical isolation units during previous viral contagions are sharing what it’s like to endure weeks cut off from the outside world.

headlines.title: Good news: You don’t need surgery. But you’ll have to apply maggots to your wound.
headlines.description: The lowly maggot gets a bad rap, mostly known for feeding on corpses and rotting meat.

headlines.title: Dangerous Teen Tanning Trend Raises Health Concerns
headlines.description: Tans lines are becoming a status symbol among teens on social media and surveys shows more than half of Gen-Zers don’t apply sunscreen. NBC’s Vicky Nguyen reports for TODAY on the impact the dangerous trend can have on skin.

headlines.title: Kyle Busch’s Death Certificate Sheds Light on His Final Days
headlines.description: Kyle Busch’s death certificate is shedding new light on what led to his sudden death after going from peak performance just one week prior to the tragedy. The certificate reportedly reveals the NASCAR great had bacterial pneumonia from “days to weeks.” NBC’s Kaylee Hartung reports and Dr. John Torres joins TODAY with medical analysis.

headlines.title: New Video Shows Devastation After Chemical Plant Implosion
headlines.description: New video captures the devastation inside the Nippon Paper Industries plant in Washington, moments after the implosion of a massive chemical tank. Authorities say two people were killed and at least nine are still missing. The cause of the disaster is still unknown. NBC’s Morgan Chesky reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: US Airports Begin Screening Flyers From Ebola-Stricken Countries
headlines.description: Four major American airports are now the exclusive entry points for any flights arriving from countries affected by the ongoing Ebola outbreak. According to the CDC, those travelers will head to a designated screening area and complete a brief questionnaire about their travel history and symptoms, have their temperature checked and undergo additional health assessments if needed. NBC’s Stephanie Gosk reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Is the Forme Power Bra really worth $179?
headlines.description: I tested the Forme Power Bra for five months to see if it could improve my posture and back support. Here’s what actually happened.

headlines.title: The best running watches, tested over hundreds of miles
headlines.description: I’ve tested dozens of running watches from Garmin, Coros, Amazfit, Fitbit, Apple and more. Here are my top picks for beginners, races and ultra-athletes alike.
